content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Verified all three amendments against source code. The double-write bug (fr-9-990fa831) and backdated resume timestamp (fr-9-b5f8f18e) are confirmed real issues with clear fixes. The paused-to-completed reconciliation (fr-9-066c158d) correctly identifies a real ordering bug, but the proposed fix is incomplete: reordering the branches would expose a new error because build_reconciled_transition_events treats Paused->Completed as an invalid transition (caught by the catch-all _ arm at line 727-732), and allows_transition_to for Paused only permits transition to Running.

## Votes

- `fr-9-990fa831`: Accept
  Verified in source: persist_plan_locked writes the snapshot at line 1593 via standalone write_snapshot, then commit_snapshot_and_journal_ops writes it again at line 153 inside its implementation. This creates a crash window where the snapshot reflects the new plan state but the journal has no corresponding event, breaking the atomicity invariant. The error context at line 1609 even acknowledges the risk. Removing the standalone write at 1593 while keeping validate_snapshot is the correct minimal fix since commit_snapshot_and_journal_ops handles both the write and rollback-on-failure atomically.
- `fr-9-066c158d`: Reject
  The ordering bug is real and verified: the Paused branch at line 907 short-circuits before the all-beads-closed completion check at lines 909-915, so milestones get stuck in Paused when the last bead completes. However, the amendment's proposed fix (reordering branches) is incomplete. Code inspection confirms that allows_transition_to for Paused only permits transition to Running (line 84-94 of the model), and the match statement in build_reconciled_transition_events has no case for (Paused, Completed), so it would hit the catch-all error at lines 727-732. A correct fix must also define the Paused->Completed transition path (likely synthesizing Paused->Running->Completed intermediates) and update the transition validator accordingly.
- `fr-9-b5f8f18e`: Accept
  Verified in source: the resume path at engine.rs:1648-1653 passes resume_state.started_at (derived from the original RunStarted event timestamp at line 6175) to sync_milestone_bead_start. This propagates through record_bead_start into build_reconciled_transition_events, where at lines 739-748 the bridge_timestamp for Running transitions uses this value. The result is that Paused->Running lifecycle events are backdated to the original run start rather than the actual resume moment, distorting journal history and duration calculations. Using the actual resume timestamp is the correct fix.
